topStories.description: In the poll-bound State, PM Narendra Modi says plantation workers had to wait years for land rights because Congress never cared for them during their tenure; this is the first such initiative in 200 years of Assam tea

topStories.title: Supreme Court declines plea to make nucleic acid tests compulsory at blood banks
topStories.description: NAT tests are more expensive but more accurate in detecting HIV, Hepatitis B, C viruses in the blood; petitioner sought to protect thalassemia patients who require frequent blood transfusions and are at risk from infected blood

topStories.title: Ukraine and allies fear easing Russian sanctions will prolong war
topStories.description: Ukraine's president says the US decision "certainly does not help achieve peace".

topStories.title: Shehbaz Sharif Reaffirms Pakistan's Support For Saudi Arabia In Talks With MBS
topStories.description: The two leaders met on Thursday in Jeddah after PM Shehbaz dashed to Saudi Arabia in the wake of the deteriorating situation in West Asia following US and Israeli attacks on Iran.

topStories.title: Manthani artist’s 3D artwork hogs limelight at international street art festival in Florida
Thousands march worldwide in solidarity with Palestine, Iran on al-Quds Day
topStories.description: Despite war, al-Quds Day marches in Tehran saw thousands protest, voicing anger against Israel and the US.

topStories.title: A president suing himself? Why experts say Trump’s $10bn lawsuit might fail
topStories.description: Experts say Trump's decision to sue over his leaked tax returns risks putting him in charge of arranging his own payout.

topStories.title: Iran’s arsenal power crushed? US Defence Secy Pete Hegseth claims ‘missiles down 90%, drones down 95%’
topStories.description: Speaking at a Pentagon press conference, Hegseth said: “Iran has no air defenses. Iran has no air force. Iran has no navy. Their missiles, their missile launchers and drones are being destroyed…”

topStories.title: Chipotle announces BOGO burritos on Friday, 13 March, in US. But here's the catch to avail the offer — details here
topStories.description: Chipotle Mexican Grill is offering a limited-time BOGO deal on March 13, from 3-4 p.m. Customers must show a tattoo to redeem the offer, which allows one free item for every purchased entrée of equal or higher value, capped at five items per bill.

topStories.title: US’s Hegseth claims new Iran Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei injured
topStories.description: Pete Hegseth's remarks come a day after Khamenei pledged to keep fighting in first statement since he was named leader.

topStories.title: Rahul Gandhi's big Kanshi Ram claim in UP Dalit outreach: 'Nehru would've made him Congress CM'
topStories.description: BSP under Kanshi Ram's protege, ex-CM Mayawati has tense relationship with Congress and SP, which are focusing on social justice agenda ahead of assembly polls

topStories.title: All six crew members killed after US refuelling plane crashes in Iraq
topStories.description: The US Central Command says all six crew members died after a refuelling aircraft went down over western Iraq.

dailyNews.title: Cuba says it will release 51 prisoners in the coming days
dailyNews.description: Havana says it is in talks with Washington as no fuel has entered the island in three months.

dailyNews.title: US’s new scramble for Africa is biomedical imperialism
dailyNews.description: From Zimbabwe to Zambia, US health deals are raising fears of a new era of biomedical extractivism.

dailyNews.title: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth claims Iran’s new Supreme Leader ‘likely wounded and disfigured’
dailyNews.description: Hegseth criticized Khamenei’s first statement for being written rather than delivered via video or audio and said there was no proof of his condition.

dailyNews.title: Cuban President Diaz-Canel says talks held with US amid Trump threats
dailyNews.description: Miguel Diaz-Canel says discussions held to find solutions 'through dialogue' as Washington tightens oil blockade.

dailyNews.title: US, Israel hit more than 15,000 targets in Iran war: Pentagon
dailyNews.description: The United States and Israel have struck more than 15,000 targets since launching a war against Iran last month, US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said on Friday.

dailyNews.title: Defiant Iran President Pezeshkian meets people on Tehran streets amid Trump warning - Video
dailyNews.description: Due to the ongoing conflict, to date, 2000 people have been killed, most of them in Iran, but many also in Lebanon and a growing number in the Gulf, reports say

dailyNews.title: Four killed in US military plane crash in Iraq
dailyNews.description: Four killed in US military plane crash in Iraq

dailyNews.title: Florida Republicans send SAVE Act–style proof-of-citizenship voting bill to DeSantis’ desk
dailyNews.description: Florida lawmakers pass strict elections bill requiring voter citizenship verification, sending measure to Gov. DeSantis who's expected to sign it.

dailyNews.title: Piers Morgan rips Megan Rapinoe as 'hypocritical' over silence on Iranian women's soccer team
dailyNews.description: Piers Morgan criticized Megan Rapinoe for her apparent silence on the Iranian women's soccer team situation, calling it hypocritical given her previous stance on women's sports.

dailyNews.title: iPhone calendar spam invites are surging
dailyNews.description: Apple iPhone users report they are seeing fake Apple Calendar spam invites about viruses and prizes, often caused by unwanted spam subscriptions.

dailyNews.title: Democrat Rep Josh Riley blasted as 'hypocrite' over ties to energy firm he criticizes on campaign trail
dailyNews.description: A competitive House race in upstate New York heats up as Republican Peter Oberacker attacks Democrat Josh Riley over his ties to a utility company.

dailyNews.title: Trump vows to hit Iran 'very hard' after obliterating nearly '90 percent' of regime missiles
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump outlines continued Iran war strategy, claims 90% of Iranian missiles destroyed and signals more strikes as tensions drive oil prices higher.

topStories.title: Chicago Teachers Union calls for school shutdown on May Day to protest Trump
topStories.description: The Chicago Teachers Union requested the May 1 school day off to engage in May Day civic action, asking Mayor Brandon Johnson and the Board of Education for their official support.

topStories.title: Pete Hegseth Says ‘the Sooner David Ellison’ Buys CNN, ‘the Better’
topStories.description: The remarks fueled concerns that CNN’s prospective new owner may shift its coverage in a Trump-friendly direction.

topStories.title: Cuba Acknowledges Talks with Trump Officials For the First Time
topStories.description: President Miguel Díaz-Canel, whose country is rapidly running out of fuel, said the talks were based on “respect for the political systems of both countries.”

topStories.title: His Harvard Lab Was Thriving. Then Came the Cuts.
topStories.description: Will Mair, who studies aging, lost almost all his research funds when the White House cracked down on Harvard. He was wholly unprepared for the upheaval that followed.

topStories.title: When a President Gets Addicted to Regime Change
topStories.description: Venezuela gave Trump a taste of success. This isn’t the first time an American president has gotten hooked on overthrowing foreign governments.

topStories.title: Mussolini Would Have Loved Trump’s Ballroom
topStories.description: Trump’s plans for Washington bring to mind what Mussolini did — and tried to do — to Rome.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls U.S. operations in Iran 'a little excursion'

dailyNews.title: Trump expects military to do 'more of the same' in Iran
dailyNews.description: Trump expects military to do 'more of the same' in Iran

dailyNews.title: 6 more U.S. deaths, vague threat from Trump as Iran war nears 2-week mark
dailyNews.description: The U.S. military says six service members were killed in a plane crash in Iraq, as Iran's continued attacks on Gulf states keep oil prices high.

dailyNews.title: Trump weighs Jones Act waiver amid rising fuel prices, White House says
dailyNews.description: Easing the century-old shipping law could help lower fuel prices as the Iran war pushes crude oil near $100 a barrel, experts say.

dailyNews.title: Trump reportedly told G7 leaders that Iran was about to surrender before supreme leader's address
dailyNews.description: President Trump told G7 leaders during a virtual meeting that Iran was "about to surrender," Axios reports. The claims apparently occurred before Iran's new Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei's address emerged. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.

dailyNews.title: 14 million doses of fentanyl  seized from secret drug lab in Mexico
dailyNews.description: The raids come as President Donald Trump ramps up his criticism of Mexico's record on fighting drug trafficking.

dailyNews.title: Trump says he thinks Putin is helping Iran
dailyNews.description: Russian leaders told President Trump they aren't sharing intelligence with Iran as it fights the U.S. and Israel, special envoy Steve Witkoff said this week.

dailyNews.title: Trump can't 'drill, baby, drill' his way out of this Iran-inspired oil crisis
dailyNews.description: Oil prices have skyrocketed in the days since President Donald Trump's war on Iran began.

dailyNews.title: Did tariff dividend checks just become more likely? Economists weigh in
dailyNews.description: Months after President Donald Trump suggested sending Americans a $2,000 tariff dividend check, there's a chance households could see that stimulus in 2026.

dailyNews.title: Oil holds near $100 as Trump says America has 'ammunition, and plenty of time’ to fight Iran war
dailyNews.description: WTI crude saw its biggest weekly gain since the 1980s last week.

dailyNews.title: Cuba’s president confirms talks with U.S. — but warns an agreement will take time
dailyNews.description: It comes at a time when the Caribbean island is grappling with a worsening economic crisis.

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: 2,200 Marines headed to Middle East
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israel strikes.
